data.title: argc parser warns on @describe tags inside heredoc template strings
data.description:
## Description

When using argc comment tags (like `@describe`, `@cmd`, etc.) inside heredoc template strings for code generation, the argc parser incorrectly interprets them as actual directives and emits warnings.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a bash script with argc that generates templates using heredocs
2. Include argc comment tags in the heredoc content (e.g., for generating template scripts)
3. Run the build process

## Example Code

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -e

# @cmd Create a new argc script template
# @option --name! Script name
create_script() {
  cat >"$argc_name.sh" <<'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -e

# @describe DESCRIPTION_PLACEHOLDER
# @option --input! Input file

main() {
    echo "Processing: $argc_input"
}

eval "$(argc --argc-eval "$0" "$@")"
EOF
}

eval "$(argc --argc-eval "$0" "$@")"
```

## Expected Behavior

Argc parser should recognize that content within heredoc blocks (especially quoted heredocs like `<<'EOF'`) is literal string content and should not be parsed for argc directives.

## Actual Behavior

Parser emits warnings:

```
@describe(line 162) shouldn't be here, @cmd is missing?
```

## Environment

* argc version: (output of `argc --argc-version`)
* Platform: Linux
* Shell: bash

## Workaround

Currently working around this by commenting out scripts that generate argc templates from the build process.

## Suggested Fix

The argc parser should skip comment tag parsing when inside heredoc blocks, particularly:

* Between `<<'EOF'` and `EOF` (quoted heredocs)
* Between `<<EOF` and `EOF` (unquoted heredocs)
* Between `<<"EOF"` and `EOF` (double-quoted heredocs)

This is a common pattern for code generation tools and template generators.

## Real-world Impact

This affects the `argc-expert` agent in the SkogAI/tools repository, which provides argc script generation and best practices. See: [https://github.com/SkogAI/tools/blob/main/agents/argc-expert/tools.sh](<https://github.com/SkogAI/tools/blob/main/agents/argc-expert/tools.sh>)
